disclaimer: Web-sourced dataset for an AI Engineering capstone. Each figure carries the source URL where it was verified and an 'accessed_date'. 'sourced' = an official/published policy figure (tuition, visa, semester fee); 'estimate' = a typical value that varies by individual (most living costs, drawn from Numbeo). Tuition/living costs change over time — verify at the cited source before deciding. Built country-by-country; see PROGRESS.md for coverage.

insurance.note: Public student health insurance (statutory, e.g. Techniker Krankenkasse). Ranges ~EUR 130-146/month depending on age and long-term-care surcharge; ~136 typical for students under 30.

METU international graduate rate ~USD 750/semester (2024-2025 official) = ~USD 1,500/yr, paid in TRY at the minimum gazetted amount. Verify the 2025-2026 figure in the linked PDF.

tuition.note: Non-EU public price EUR 84.07/credit ('other master's' band) x 60 ECTS = EUR 5,044 for the one-year programme. EU/EEA students pay EUR 45.02/credit.

work.note: Higher-education students may work up to 30 h/week under Royal Decree 1155/2024 (in force May 2025). Wage derived from the 2025 SMI (minimum wage) of EUR 1,184/month x 14 payments = EUR 16,576/yr, ~EUR 8.63/h.

insurance.note: Registration with the French student social security (Assurance Maladie, via etudiant-etranger.ameli.fr) is free and covers ~70% of medical costs. An optional complementary mutuelle (~EUR 50-200/yr) tops up the rest.

work.note: Non-EU students may work up to 60% of full-time (964 h/year, ~20 h/week) on their residence permit. Statutory minimum wage (SMIC) 2025 = EUR 11.65 gross/hour.
